title: 7 Herbal Tincture Remedies for Dry Eyes and Itchy Irritation

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the delicate tissues in the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome.
Kaempferol is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry eye symptoms.
Tannins are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
Polyphenols are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, promoting healthy tear production and lubrication.
Rutin is good for dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the eyes, thereby soothing and relieving dry, irritated eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help with dry eyes. The main ingredient, Euphrasia, is a plant that has been used for centuries to soothe and calm eye irritations. When taken as a tincture, it can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, making it easier to produce tears. This is especially helpful for people who have dry eyes due to certain medical conditions or side effects from medication. By increasing tear production, Euphrasia officinalis tincture can help alleviate symptoms of dry eyes, such as itchiness, redness, and blurred vision. The tincture works by stimulating the lacrimal gland, which is responsible for producing tears, and can help restore the natural balance of the eyes' moisture.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal tincture for dry eyes, first gather dried or fresh flowers, leaves, and stems of the plant. If using fresh, let them dry completely in a warm, dry place, then chop them finely. Combine 1 part of the dried Achillea millefolium with 2 parts of a solvent, like vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. The jar should be filled to the top to allow the liquid to fully extract the plant's properties. Close the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called maceration, and it helps to draw out the plant's healing properties into the liquid. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Achillea millefolium tincture is now ready to use. For dry eyes, take a few drops of the tincture under your tongue or add it to a lubricating eye drop according to the package instructions.
remedies.image_alt: Euphrasia officinalis herbal tinctures for dry eyes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and soothe dry, irritated eyes.

Flavonoids, also known as Yarrow, are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing irritation and promoting healthy tear production.
Polysaccharides are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, soothing and moisturizing them naturally.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tincture is good for dry eyes because it helps soothe and calm the eyes. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, which can make dry eyes feel even worse. The tincture also has hydrating properties that help lock in moisture, keeping the eyes moist and comfortable. This is especially helpful when you have dry eyes because it can help keep the eyes from feeling gritty or itchy. Aloe vera tincture can also help protect the eyes from irritation and damage caused by dry air, pollution, or other environmental factors. Using aloe vera tincture can provide quick relief from dry, irritated eyes and help you feel more comfortable throughout the day.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for dry eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried Achillea millefolium flowers and leaves. If using fresh herbs, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Once dry, combine 1 part of the dried herbs with 2 parts of a high-proof spirit, such as vodka or rum, in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily to help the herbs infuse into the spirit. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. To use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and apply it to the eyes with a cotton ball or swab 2-3 times a day. The Achillea millefolium tincture may help soothe and moisturize the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal tinctures for dry eyes

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tincture is a natural remedy that can help soothe and heal dry, irritated eyes. This is because calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. The tincture can be applied directly to the eyes with a cotton ball, or mixed with water and used as eye drops. Calendula's soothing effects help calm the eyes, reducing discomfort and grittiness. It also has antimicrobial properties that prevent infections and promote healing. Additionally, calendula tincture may help strengthen the tear ducts, allowing the eyes to produce more tears and stay moist. This makes it easier to get rid of dry, itchy eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal tincture for dry eyes, you'll need fresh or dried yarrow flowers, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or glycerin, and a dark-colored glass bottle for storage. First, gather or purchase yarrow flowers and chop them finely to release their medicinal properties. Fill the glass jar about 1/4 full with the chopped yarrow flowers, and then pour vodka or glycerin over the flowers, making sure they're completely covered. Secure the lid tightly and let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help release the herbal properties into the liquid. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark-colored glass bottle, discarding the solids. Label the bottle with the date, herbal name, and any instructions for use. This yarrow tincture can be taken as needed to help soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes - simply put a few drops under the tongue or add to water, and then apply a cold compress to the eyes for added relief.

Alkaloids have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and soothe the eyes, making it a good natural remedy for people who suffer from dry eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tincture can help with dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it reduces swelling and redness in the eyes. The tincture also contains compounds that soothe and calm the eyes, making it easier to open them and get rid of dryness. Additionally, Symphytum officinale tincture can help to heal the delicate tissues around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort. Some people use it because it may also improve the production of tears, which helps to keep the eyes moist and comfortable. The tincture can be applied directly to the eyes with a cotton ball, or taken internally to get the benefits of its healing properties.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for dry eyes, start by harvesting fresh or dried Achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and stems from a trusted source. Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine one part of the dried plant material with one part of a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a clean glass jar. For a stronger tincture, use a higher ratio of plant material to oil. Secure the lid tightly and allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the steeping period, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If desired, add a small amount of water or a glycerin base to the tincture to enhance its solubility. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed to help alleviate dry eye symptoms. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or take prescription medications.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal tinctures for dry eyes

Flavonoids are good for dry eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the delicate tissues in the eyes, reducing redness and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a liquid extract from the root of the licorice plant. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes. The tincture can also help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making it a good option for people who experience dry eye symptoms. The anti-inflammatory compounds in the tincture can help to reduce the production of chemicals that cause inflammation in the eyes. This can help to reduce discomfort and promote healing. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to promote overall eye health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that may help with dry eyes, you'll need some dried yarrow flowers, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, fill the glass jar about 1/4 full with the dried yarrow flowers, making sure they're not packed too tightly. Next, pour the carrier oil over the yarrow flowers, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part dried yarrow to 3-4 parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the tincture extract from the yarrow. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If desired, add a solvent like vodka or glycerin to the liquid, usually in a 1:1 ratio, to help the tincture dissolve in water. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water to help soothe dry eyes, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a natural remedy that may help with dry eyes. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the eyes and make them feel better. The tincture's soothing effects can calm irritated eyes and reduce redness. Echinacea purpurea is also rich in antioxidants, which can protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. This can help keep the eyes healthy and functioning properly. Some people find that using Echinacea purpurea tincture helps to improve tear production, which can help to keep the eyes moist and comfortable. Additionally, the tincture may help to reduce the risk of eye infections, which can be a complication of dry eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that helps with dry eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves and flowers of the plant, also known as yarrow. Combine 1 part of the plant material with 2 parts of a neutral-tasting liquid like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Make sure the plant material is completely covered by the liquid, and then seal the jar tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every day or two to help the plant release its properties into the liquid. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material. You now have a strong tincture that can be diluted with water to make a treatment for dry eyes. To use it, simply add 5-10 drops of the tincture to your eyes 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tincture is good for dry eyes because it helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The plant's natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and redness, making it easier for eyes to heal. Its antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection and promote healthy eye tissue. Additionally, Achillea millefolium contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that act as antioxidants, protecting eyes from damage caused by free radicals. When applied to the eyes, the tincture's cooling and moistening properties help to hydrate and calm dry, itchy eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting clear vision. This natural remedy can be used to treat a range of eye problems, including dry eye syndrome, conjunctivitis, and even minor eye injuries.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Achillea millefolium herbal tincture that may help with dry eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried Achillea millefolium flowers, leaves, and stems. Use a ratio of one part plant material to five parts solvent, such as 80-proof vodka or glycerin. Combine the plant material and solvent in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly. Place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily. This allows the plant's active compounds to infuse into the solvent. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the plant material. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed. Typically, a dropperful of the tincture is added to water or taken sublingually (under the tongue) to help soothe and moisturize dry eyes.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for dry eyes

intro_desc: Dry eyes can be a real nuisance, making everyday tasks feel like a struggle. But, there's a natural solution that can help: herbal tinctures. Two herbs that are particularly effective in soothing dry eyes are Euphrasia officinalis, also known as Eyebright, and Aloe vera. Euphrasia officinalis is great for reducing inflammation and fighting off infections that can contribute to dry eyes. Its anti-inflammatory properties help calm the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. Aloe vera, on the other hand, is a natural moisturizer that helps replenish the tears and keep the eyes hydrated. It's also antibacterial, which helps prevent infections that can make dry eyes worse.  Fixing dry eyes is crucial because it can affect your quality of life. When your eyes are dry and irritated, it's hard to focus on anything else. Simple tasks like watching TV, reading, or even driving can become a challenge. And, if left untreated, dry eyes can lead to more serious problems like vision loss. By using herbal tinctures like Euphrasia officinalis and Aloe vera, you can alleviate dry eyes and get back to living your life without discomfort. You'll be able to focus on the things you love, without the distraction of dry, irritated eyes. And, with regular use, you may even find that your eyes feel more comfortable and refreshed overall.
supplementary_best: For dry eyes, a combination of Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, and Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures is recommended. Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eye flower, is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are known to soothe and protect the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Calendula officinalis contains triterpenes and sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to calm and protect the eye area. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is a gentle demulcent that provides moisture and soothes dry, irritated eyes. This combination works well together because Euphrasia officinalis addresses the root cause of dryness, Calendula officinalis reduces inflammation, and Glycyrrhiza glabra provides soothing moisture, creating a balanced and effective remedy for dry eyes.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry eyes, you should avoid using herbal tinctures that can dry out your eyes further or make the problem worse. Some examples of these herbs include Eyebright's close relative, 'Verbascum thapsus' (Mullein), which can cause further irritation and dryness. 'Capsicum annuum' (Cayenne pepper) and 'Gelsemium sempervirens' can also be a problem. 'Capsicum annuum' can make your eyes more sensitive, while 'Gelsemium sempervirens' can reduce tear production and cause dryness. 'Krameria triandra' (Rhatany) may also not be suitable, as it can cause dryness and irritation. 'Sanguinaria canadensis' (Bloodroot) should also be used with caution, as it can cause irritation and potentially exacerbate dry eye problems. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have pre-existing eye conditions.
supplementary_other_remedies: Omega-3 fatty acids found in fish oil supplements can help with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting the health of the tear glands. The fatty acids also help to keep the eyes moist and comfortable. Omega-3 supplements have been shown to improve tear quality and reduce dry eye symptoms.  Another natural remedy is eye drops made from pure, cold-pressed coconut oil. The oil's fatty acids help to lubricate and soothe dry eyes. It also contains lauric acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm irritated eyes.  Warm compresses made from clean, damp cloths can also help to alleviate dry eyes. Soaking a cloth in warm water and applying it to the eyes can help to loosen up blockages in the tear ducts and increase tear production. This can provide relief from dry eye symptoms and help to keep the eyes feeling moist and comfortable.  Aloe vera gel, often used to soothe skin irritations, can also be applied to the eyes to help with dryness. The gel's anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties can help to calm and hydrate the eyes, reducing dry eye symptoms and discomfort.  Eye exercises, such as rolling the eyes or massaging the eyelids, can also help to stimulate tear production and alleviate dry eye symptoms. These exercises can help to increase blood flow to the eyes and promote the health of the tear glands.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry eyes often experience other eye problems. One common issue is blurred vision or double vision. This happens because the eyes are not producing enough tears to keep them moist and clear. Medicinal herbs like Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) can help. Eyebright has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, allowing them to produce more tears and improving vision.  Another issue people with dry eyes may face is sensitivity to light. This is because the eyes are irritated and cannot filter out harsh light as well as they should. Bilberry (Vaccinium myrtillus) can help. Bilberry is rich in antioxidants that protect the eyes from damage and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the eyes to adjust to light.  Red, itchy, or gritty eyes are also common in people with dry eyes. This is because the eyes are irritated and not producing enough lubrication. Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis) can help. Marshmallow root has soothing properties that calm irritated eyes and reduce inflammation, making them feel more comfortable.  Dry eyes can also lead to eye infections, like conjunctivitis or blepharitis. These infections occur when bacteria or viruses infect the eyes, making them red, itchy, and painful. Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) can help. Goldenseal has antibacterial properties that fight off infections and reduce inflammation in the eyes.  Eye fatigue is another issue people with dry eyes may experience. This is because the eyes are working harder to produce tears and focus on objects. Ginkgo biloba (Ginkgo biloba) can help. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the eyes, reducing eye fatigue and improving vision.  Lastly, people with dry eyes may experience headaches or migraines. This is because the eyes are irritated and causing tension in the face and head. Feverfew (Tanacetum parthenium) can help. Feverfew has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce tension and pain in the head and face, making it easier to manage headaches and migraines.
